typecheck.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m mypy app/ src/ --ignore-missing-imports
typecheck.detail: Success: no issues found in 23 source files

lint.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m ruff check src/ app/
lint.detail: All checks passed (after fixing 1 W293 whitespace issue)

appliedUpdates.description: Created .factory/library/frequi-compat.md documenting FreqUI polling endpoints (including sysinfo), show_config required fields (including timerange), known display quirks, CSI 300 data seeding requirement, and frontend build requirement after fresh clone.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update 'API Compatibility Requirements' section: add /sysinfo to the list of FreqUI polling endpoints (alongside /whitelist, /blacklist, /status, /profit). Also document that show_config must include a 'timerange' field (FreqUI's TimeRangeSelect.vue calls .split() on it).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature frequi-backtest-integration discovered both issues via browser console errors. Worker had to add /api/v1/sysinfo endpoint and timerange field to show_config (commit bfca54a) to eliminate JS errors.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: data-layer objects (MarketReader, BacktestStore, IndexFetcher) should be instantiated as module-level singletons in API endpoint files, not re-created per request. This matches the existing pattern in backtest.py and pairs.py.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature benchmark-comparison used per-request IndexFetcher() instantiation with lazy import inside function body, while existing code uses module-level _store = BacktestStore() and _reader = MarketReader(). Inconsistency flagged in reviews for both frequi-backtest-integration and benchmark-comparison.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate services.yaml in quant-dashboard/.factory/ vs root .factory/
rejectedObservations.reason: The quant-dashboard-level services.yaml was likely created by an early worker before the root-level one existed. Not a systemic issue — the root .factory/services.yaml is the canonical one used by the mission system.

rejectedObservations.observation: dist/ excluded by .gitignore means build step required after fresh clone
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in .factory/services.yaml via build_frontend command, and now also documented in the new .factory/library/frequi-compat.md. Not a gap.
